div.h2: Algorithm Steps: H0..H7 Initial Values — Square Root Derivation

ol.li: Extract the fractional part: frac(√p) = √p − floor(√p)

div.p: 🔒 Security property: Every constant is independently verifiable using only a calculator or python3 -c "import math; hex(int((math.sqrt(2)%1)*2**32))". No hidden structure, no trapdoor, no designer choice — the derivation procedure is more constrained than the space of backdoor-enabling values. Contrast with Dual_EC_DRBG (NSA backdoor via unverifiable constants).
Verify H[0] yourself: python import math v = math.sqrt(2) % 1 # 0.41421356237… i = int(v * 2**32) # 1779033703 hex(i) # '0x6a09e667' ✓
